document.title: Software Development Plan for HnX-P1, HnX-PB Portable X-ray System

sourceSummary.oneLine: Manufacturer software development plan for HnX-P1 and HnX-PB portable X-ray system software.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines software development lifecycle, classification, requirements, risk management, verification and validation, release, configuration management, problem handling, and maintenance activities.
keyPoints: The plan applies to Portable X-ray system software for HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
FDA level of concern assessment indicates Moderate level based on Table 2 answers.
EN 62304 software safety classification criteria are described as Class A, Class B, and Class C.
Development follows Classic waterfall methodology and references a V model relationship between requirements and test documents.
Software verification and validation include unit test, integration, and system test.
Software changes require validation before application.

concepts.name: Software Development Plan
concepts.evidence: This plan outlines the tasks, responsibilities, resources, and other items pertinent to the specific development project.

concepts.name: FDA Level of Concern
concepts.evidence: S/W is classified into Major, Moderate and Minor by its level of concern

concepts.name: Moderate Level of Concern
concepts.evidence: If the Software Device is not Major Level of Concern and the answer to any one question below is Yes, the Level of Concern is likely to be Moderate.

concepts.name: EN 62304 software safety class
concepts.evidence: The software safety classes shall initially be assigned based on concern as follows: -. Class A : There is no injury nor health damage. -. Class B : The possible harm is non-serious injury. -. Class C : The possible harm is death or serious injury.

concepts.name: Classic waterfall methodology
concepts.evidence: Software development process of H&abyz Co., Ltd. follows “Classic waterfall methodology"

concepts.name: V Model of Software Development
concepts.evidence: Figure: “V” Model of Software Development depicts the relationship between requirements and test documents and reports.

concepts.name: Software Requirements Specification
concepts.evidence: Function by each module and essential factor that each function must equip in software are as below

concepts.name: Software Design Specification
concepts.evidence: The software design specification explains materialization of the requirements on the software device.

concepts.name: Software Risk Management
concepts.evidence: As for the software risk management, personnel who participated in the program development process shall decide, analyze potential risk, reason, probability and severity of the software.

concepts.name: Software Verification and Validation
concepts.evidence: Software verification and validation shall be confirmed through the software test. The software test shall conduct the unit test, integration and system test.

concepts.name: Software Configuration Management
concepts.evidence: In case the software configuration is changed, it should be validated before it is applied to the software.

concepts.name: Software Maintenance
concepts.evidence: This chapter provides a recommended schedule for periodic maintenance of the HnX-P1

evidence.claim: The document is a Software Development Plan for HnX-P1 and HnX-PB Portable X-ray System.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Development Plan for HnX-P1, HnX-PB Portable X-ray System

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-SDP-HNX Rev.2.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-SDP-HNX (Rev.2)

evidence.claim: The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on May 05, 2025.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared Software team Song, Jinseon May 05, 2025 Review Software team manager Lee, kyungSoo May 05, 2025 Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu May 05, 2025

evidence.claim: Revision 2 was dated May 04, 2025 and added variant model HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: 2 May 04, 2025 Add variant model (HnX-PB)

evidence.claim: The software for HnX-P1 and HnX-PB is described as the same software with different names.
evidence.evidenceText: (HnX_P1 and HnX_PB, the software for HnX-P1 and HnX-PB, are the same software with different names.)

evidence.claim: The plan states that software classification complies with FDA Guideline.
evidence.evidenceText: Software classification comply with FDA Guide Line

evidence.claim: The software device is not classified as Major level of concern by the checklist answers.
evidence.evidenceText: If the answer to any one question below is Yes, the Level of Concern for the Software Device is likely to be “Major”. Answer ... □Y ■N

evidence.claim: The software device is indicated as Moderate level of concern based on Table 2 Yes answers.
evidence.evidenceText: If the Software Device is not Major Level of Concern and the answer to any one question below is Yes, the Level of Concern is likely to be Moderate . Answer 1. Is the Software Device an accessory to a medical device that has a Moderate Level of Concern? ■Y □N

evidence.claim: The plan describes EN 62304 software safety classes A, B, and C.
evidence.evidenceText: The software safety classes shall initially be assigned based on concern as follows: -. Class A : There is no injury nor health damage. -. Class B : The possible harm is non-serious injury. -. Class C : The possible harm is death or serious injury.

evidence.claim: The plan states that Class C requirements apply until a software safety class is assigned.
evidence.evidenceText: [If applicable] For each software system, until a software safety class is assigned, Class C requirements applied.

evidence.claim: The software development process follows Classic waterfall methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process of H&abyz Co., Ltd. follows “Classic waterfall methodology"

evidence.claim: The development process uses analysis, planning, design, implementation, qualification, and maintenance steps.
evidence.evidenceText: Step Main Contents Analysis ... Planning ... Design ... Implementation ... Qualification ... Maintenance

evidence.claim: The software development resources identify C/C++ as the program language.
evidence.evidenceText: Resources Section Resource Program Language C/C++

evidence.claim: The MCU model identified for development resources is STM32H7A3VG.
evidence.evidenceText: Resources Section Resource Program Language C/C++ MCU Model STM32H7A3VG

evidence.claim: Functional requirements include configuration, calibration, data acquisition, APR, X-ray irradiation control, charger, battery, error checking, Bluetooth, display control, and SID modules.
evidence.evidenceText: Development Configuration module ... Development Calibration module ... Development Data acquisition module ... Development APR module ... Development Filament preheating and X-ray irradiation, Lamp control module ... Development Charger module ... Development Battery module ... Development Checking error module ... Development Bluetooth function module ... Development Display control module ... Development SID module

evidence.claim: Software risk analysis references EN ISO14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Software risk analysis Identification of the characteristics and hazards associated with the intended use and the safety of the device, risk assessment EN ISO14971:2019 Software Validation Report Quality manager

evidence.claim: Software development planning references EN ISO13485:2016.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development Devise a plan for the software development and put it in writing EN ISO13485:2016 Software development plan R&D manager

evidence.claim: Software verification and validation are confirmed through software testing including unit, integration, and system tests.
evidence.evidenceText: Software verification and validation shall be confirmed through the software test. The software test shall conduct the unit test, integration and system test.

evidence.claim: Software release requires verification completion and evaluation of results before release.
evidence.evidenceText: It should be ensured that the software verification is completed and that the results are evaluated before releasing the software.

evidence.claim: Identified residual abnormal conditions must be documented and evaluated for unacceptable risk.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual abnormalities should be documented. In order to ensure that all identified residual abnormalities are not causing unacceptable risks, It should be ensured that such abnormal conditions have been evaluated.

evidence.claim: Software configuration changes must be recorded, managed in the Software Validation Report, and validated before application.
evidence.evidenceText: In case the software configuration is changed, it should be recorded, managed on 12. Software Configuration Management of the Software Validation Report . In case the software configuration is changed, it should be validated before it is applied to the software.

evidence.claim: Software issues must be recorded and applicable corrective measures conducted.
evidence.evidenceText: Any issues identified with the software shall be recorded and applicable corrective measures shall be conducted.

evidence.claim: The software must be used only after required validation has completed when correcting software issues.
evidence.evidenceText: The software shall be used only after the required validation has completed.

evidence.claim: Maintenance includes security patches every 1 year or when a security vulnerability is discovered.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Security Patch Every 1years or When a security vulnerability is discovered

evidence.claim: HnX_P1 software cannot be changed or troubleshooted in the field.
evidence.evidenceText: * HnX_P1 software cannot be changed or troubleshooted in the field. If a problem occurs, software change or troubleshooting must be done through the service center or manufacturer.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: source metadata signals FDA and the text references FDA Guideline, but the document appears to be a manufacturer software development plan rather than an FDA-issued document.
Document date has two relevant dates: approval/preparation date May 05, 2025 and Rev.2 revision date May 04, 2025.
Maintenance chapter scope says HnX-P1, while the document title and revision history include both HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
Text contains OCR or formatting artifacts in tables and headings, but key metadata and claims remain readable.
